what_it_does: A tentative design-capture document defining the Registry-Filling Pattern (GC2): a reusable production-line for growing registries. It names the chain skeleton (SOURCE -> GROUND -> MAP -> DEDUPE -> FLOOR -> JURY -> SURFACE -> [Tim APPROVES] -> WRITE-BACK), explains what each stage requires and the specific failure each prevents, lists the per-instance parameters (the reuse axes), and records three related extensions (GC1 the FLOW registry/MCP consequence already built, GC3 addresses-as-accumulation-points, and standing/next instances).
observations: Marked TENTATIVE, written 2026-06-10, explicitly states Tim has not reviewed it and assumptions are marked
Opens with Tim's verbatim intent about the high reusability of the registry-filling principle
Defines the chain in one sentence and as an 8-stage pipeline diagram
For each stage describes what it REQUIRES, citing RG10 instance numbers (940 candidates, ~550 unique after dedupe)
The MAP stage names four reusable context-package slots: the unit, parent/neighbour records, the ground, exemplars+closed-vocabularies
States the law that a model-judgement step is only designed when its context package is designed with it

A table maps each stage to the observed (not hypothetical) failure it prevents, citing concrete RG10 failures (invented INB-focus/NODE-act ids passing the prompt instruction; the all-940-fail reporting 'done, 0 proposals')
Lists six per-instance parameters as the reuse axes and asserts everything else should exist ONCE

GC1 section: the FLOW registry (runtime/flows.py + flows/<id>.py + flows MCP tool) is BUILT 2026-06-10, with repo-AUTHORED / MCP-INVOKED boundary and proposes_only enforced; founding rows registry_generation, transcript_mine, pattern_cluster
GC3 section: addresses as accumulation points — WRITE-BACK becomes a standing write-path where field families accumulate on the same address, with open design questions

Standing instances: RG10/ui-addresses (231 confirmed / 320 flagged), the transcript miner (pattern-shaped, missing floor+gate), and next candidates (feature inventory, capability vocab, mark_types/relation_types, model-capability registry)
standouts: The failure-prevention table grounds every stage in an observed failure rather than theory — e.g. the FLOOR caught real invented ids despite a verbatim copy-instruction, proving prompt context reduces but does not stop fiction
The explicit separation of what changes per instance (six parameters) from what should exist exactly ONCE (the skeleton, dedupe, jury, gate, batch state) — the reuse contract

GC1's one-door split: data-chains go through save_cascade (cascades); code-chains go through repo-authored flows — and the observation that agents default to the ungrounded design even with the grounded module in their own history
conventions: Follows the propose-only / operator-gated truth-growth discipline (nothing writes itself)
Uses the closed-vocabulary + exemplars grounding convention for small models
Marks tentativeness and assumptions explicitly per the repo's epistemic-humility convention for unreviewed design docs
concerns: Self-flagged as unreviewed by Tim with marked assumptions (e.g. ASSUMPTION that memory-writes stay ungated) — a design seed, not a settled spec
notes: This is the reusable-pattern abstraction extracted from the RG10 run — the named 8-stage registry-growing production line, what each stage prevents, the six reuse axes, and the FLOW-registry consequence already built to enforce grounding.
questions: Has Tim reviewed and confirmed the pattern, especially the ASSUMPTION that memory-writes stay ungated?
Were the open GC3 design questions (field-family naming, versioning vs in-place, per-consumer projections) resolved?
Which of the next-candidate registries (feature inventory, capability vocab, etc.) have been instantiated through this pattern?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is the tentative design capture of a reusable registry-filling pattern, and the body delivers the pattern definition, stage requirements, failure table, reuse parameters, and related extensions.
apparent_themes: Deterministic floor as the no-fiction guarantee
Grounding small models with designed context packages
intention_signals: Trying to crystallize the proven RG10 run into a reusable production-line abstraction so that future registries that lack their data entries can be grown the same way once, with the load-bearing pieces (floor, gate, ground) named and the per-instance axes isolated.
novelty: The framing of WRITE-BACK evolving from a terminal stage into a standing write-path (GC3 address accumulation), and the explicit law that a model-judgement step is only designed alongside its context package, with the four named reusable context slots.
contribution: Contributes the named, staged, reuse-parameterized registry-filling pattern with a failure-grounded justification per stage and the address-accumulation extension — the abstraction that makes the RG10 chain reusable across many registries.
summary_for_embedding: A tentative design document defining the reusable Registry-Filling Pattern (GC2): an 8-stage SOURCE-to-WRITE-BACK production line for growing registries with a deterministic floor, model jury, operator gate, six per-instance reuse parameters, and address-accumulation extension.
intention_for_embedding: Written to crystallize the proven RG10 registry-generation run into a reusable, parameterized production-line pattern for growing any under-filled registry while keeping the floor, the operator gate, and grounding load-bearing.
